name: overlay_equals_topological
description: Returns whether the current feature's geometry is topologically equal to at least one feature's geometry from a target layer, or returns an array of expression-based results for the features in the target layer whose geometry is topologically equal to the current feature's geometry. See also 'equals_topological' function.

arguments.description: the layer whose overlay is checked
an optional expression to evaluate on the features from the target layer. If not set, the function will just return a boolean indicating whether there is at least one match.
an optional expression to filter the target features to check. If not set, all the features will be checked.
an optional integer to limit the number of matching features. If not set, all the matching features will be returned.
set this to true to build a local spatial index (most of the time, this is unwanted, unless you are working with a particularly slow data provider)
Available geometry backend implementations: 'GEOS'. 'GEOS' works only on 2D components, Z or M components are ignored.

examples.returns: TRUE if the current feature's geometry is topologically the same as the geometry of a region
TRUE if the current feature's geometry is topologically the same as the geometry of a region whose population is greater than 10000
an array of names, for the regions topologically equal to the current feature
an array of names, for the regions topologically equal to the current feature
a string as a comma separated list of names, for the regions topologically equal to the current feature
an ordered array of names, for the regions topologically equal to the current feature and with a population greater than 10000
an array of geometries (in WKT), for up to two regions topologically equal to the current feature

notes: This function uses topological equality of geometries, see also 'equals_topological' function examples.
